name: copilot-cli-capability-gaps
description: CopilotCLIChatModel is the same text-bridge idea shelled to `copilot -p`, with two capability gaps worked around: (1) no `--tools none` equivalent — tool execution is genuinely prevented only by `--excluded-tools=<every built-in tool name>` plus `--disable-builtin-mcps` (an empty `--available-tools=` alone was verified NOT to stop real shell execution; excluded-tool hallucinations show up as prose the JSON protocol parser ignores); (2) no `--append-system-prompt`/`--fork-session` — system+user text fold into one combined -p argument and each role's session starts cold rather than forking from a shared root, keeping only per-role --resume reuse.
invariants: _EXCLUDED_TOOLS must track the CLI's full built-in tool list — any tool missing from it is executable on this host
resolved_model/premium_requests are read off the CLI's JSONL event stream because with model='auto' our own model= field would just log the literal string 'auto' forever
contracts: The last assistant.message event in the JSONL stream is the model's final reply for the call
